differential_diagnoses: Primary Angiitis of the Central Nervous System (PACNS)
Leptomeningeal metastasis (e.g., primary CNS lymphoma, melanoma)
Chronic lymphocytic inflammation with pontine perivascular enhancement responsive to steroids (CLIPPERS)
next_steps: Brain and meningeal biopsy (gold standard for inflammatory/neoplastic etiology)
Repeat MRI of the brain with gadolinium and vessel wall imaging
Whole-body PET/CT to evaluate for systemic occult malignancy or inflammatory process
Serum testing for autoantibodies (e.g., anti-AQP4, anti-MOG, ANA, ENA, ANCA, ACE levels)
Consider diagnostic trial of high-dose corticosteroids if biopsy is delayed or non-diagnostic
Genetic testing for familial cerebral amyloid angiopathy (e.g., APP, CST3, ITM2B mutations)
